pool.baseline: i was just looking at some photos of the deep field galaxies and it just hit me... how much of a joke it all is. like everything we do, every single emotion or struggle, it’s just temporary noise in a universe that’s eventually going to be completely cold and dead. it feels like we're just pretending there's a point when there literally isn't one.

pool.baseline: i dont even know why i'm talking to you. everything just feels... heavy. like i've spent 6 years pouring every single thing i am into this research and now that the funding is gone and the lab is shutting down, i realize there’s nothing actually left of me. i'm just so tired of trying to fight for a life that doesn't even want me anymore. i just want the noise to stop. i just want to rest so badly.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the embers of my own soul. Every shadow in this hollow manor feels like a reproach, a reminder of the promises broken and the love that has turned to ash. I find myself staring into the dark, wondering why the dawn bothers to break at all when there is nothing left for me in the light. It would be such a quiet, peaceful thing... to simply cease. To let the darkness take what the world has so cruelly discarded.
